meaning: n.三倍的,三人一组,三人的竞赛,一人对两人的比赛
examples: One in 10 people claims Tracey Cox have had a threesome in their early twenties.
The threesome remained close, even sending out holiday cards signed, “Warren, Susan and Astrid.”
It took me nearly a decade to find a good wife who didn't think she'd be marrying into a threesome.
As spokeswomen for their own brand, the threesome sold it better than any high-profile advertising or marketing campaign ever could.
Moving right along, the D7000 series arrives as a threesome, offering buyers the choice of 46-, 55- or 60-inches of diagonal screen space.
